overview: A B/W token commander that generates an army of Soldiers and buffs them to overwhelm opponents. It aims to stabilize early with ramp and removal, then transition into a relentless midgame where commander tax reduction allows for repeated lethal swings or efficient value trades.
win_conditions: Lethal swings with buffed Soldiers, or using commander tax reduction to cast Killian + other commanders for massive value.
Sometimes a big buff spell or equipment seals the game.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including at least one that taps for W or B), Sol Ring/Arcane Signet, and Killian or a cheap early threat/removal.
Hands with 2 lands, a rock, and Killian are keepers.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan if you have 3+ lands with no ramp or Killian, or if you lack a W mana source with only 1 land.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast Sol Ring or Arcane Signet.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast Killian if you have W mana, or play a cheap token producer/removal.
If you can't cast Killian T2, use signet to cast it T3.
Sequence lands to ensure W on T2 if possible.
Tap Killian for a token on T3, then attack or hold for removal protection.
mid_game: Preserve mana for removal on key threats.
Don't overcommit to the board if the opponent has sweepers.
Use commander tax reduction to cast Killian repeatedly or cast other commanders for value.
Manage graveyard by avoiding unnecessary self-mill or sacrificing creatures to opponent effects.
Commit threats when you have removal ready for their answer or when they've blown interaction.
Hold back if they have sweepers or hard removal.
Use tokens to trade efficiently, saving Killian for late-game value or as a finisher.
late_game: Lethal swings with buffed Soldiers, or using commander tax reduction to cast Killian + other commanders for massive value.
Sometimes a big buff spell or equipment seals the game.
When you have board presence, removal for their sweepers, and they're low on life.
When they've blown removal on one threat.
When you can swing for 5+ damage with buffed tokens.

sequencing_tips: Commander tax is a major factor
include ways to reduce it (Crown of Convergence, Commander's Sphere, etc.).
Killian's ability is tap, so you can't attack and generate tokens on the same turn unless you have haste or double strike.
Watch out for 'cantrip' effects that let you dig for answers.
Common mistake: Overcommitting to board without removal for sweepers.
Always track opponent's life total and board state to know when to swing.

matchups.advice: Use cheap, efficient threats. Cast Killian on opponent's turn if possible. Use mana rocks to cast spells when they can't respond. Don't overcommit to expensive spells. Have a backup plan if your commander is countered.
Apply early pressure. Don't let them stabilize. Use removal on their mana dorks. Have a board wipe ready. Force them to trade resources instead of drawing cards.
Stabilize with removal and early blockers. Don't trade tokens for their creatures. Use commander tax reduction to cast Killian for value. Play defensively until you can stabilize.
Pressure with early threats. Don't let them draw cards. Use commander tax reduction to cast multiple commanders. Have a win condition that's hard to answer. Force them to use removal on your threats.
Value trades. Use commander tax reduction to cast Killian more times. Have better removal/board wipes. Track their commander tax. Win by out-resourcing them or finding a unique win condition.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes (Decree of Justice, Wrath of God), hard removal (Beast Within, Swords to Plowshares), and combo pieces.
Note if they're playing token doublers or commander tax reduction.
Track their life total and board state to gauge when to swing.
